examples: Neither must we attempt to confine the Platonic dialogue on the Procrustes bed of a single idea.
His latest book is The Bed of Procrustes: Philosophical and Practical Aphorisms (Random House: 2010).
Procrustes was a nasty little fellow from Greek mythology who would invite guests into his home and then either stretch or amputate parts of their legs to make them fit just right in his guest bed.
